cards.title: Father of Internet Vinton Cerf Retires from Google
cards.body: Vinton Cerf, co-creator of internet protocols, is stepping down as Google's chief internet evangelist. His departure marks the end of an era for one of the internet's founding figures.

cards.title: Trump Administration Lifts Anthropic Model Restrictions
cards.body: The Trump administration dropped restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models. The move reflects ongoing policy uncertainty around AI regulation and governance.

cards.title: Wayve Launches $85M Employee Tender Offer
cards.body: Autonomous vehicle startup Wayve announced an $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation. The move reflects a growing trend of AI startups using tenders for talent retention.

cards.title: OpenClaw Arrives on Android and iOS
cards.body: The free, open-source agentic program OpenClaw is now available on mobile platforms. The launch extends AI agent capabilities directly to smartphones.

cards.title: Ex-DeepMind Researchers Launch $500M EquiLibre
cards.body: Three former DeepMind researchers founded EquiLibre Technologies, now valued at $500M+. The Prague-based lab applies poker AI expertise to quantitative hedge fund trading.

cards.title: Google Launches Nano Banana 2 Lite Image Generator
cards.body: Google released an updated, faster, and cheaper image generator for creators. The new Nano Banana 2 Lite improves accessibility for AI content production.

cards.title: Etched Hits $5B Valuation, $1B in Contracts
cards.body: AI chip competitor Etched reached $5B valuation with $1B in booked contracts. The startup positions itself as a viable alternative to Nvidia for inference systems.

cards.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 Model
cards.body: Claude Sonnet 5 delivers stronger agentic capabilities at lower pricing than Opus and Gemini Pro. The model emphasizes improved safety and cost-effectiveness for agent deployment.

cards.title: Acti Embeds AI Agents in Smartphone Keyboards
cards.body: Startup Acti launched an AI-powered keyboard for iOS and Android with cross-app functionality. Users can create custom AI shortcuts using natural language inputs.

cards.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Science Workbench
cards.body: Claude Science provides scientists with a unified environment for computational research. The workbench eliminates the need to juggle multiple databases, pipelines, and tools.

cards.title: X Launches MCP Server for AI Developers
cards.body: X released a hosted MCP server to streamline AI application integration with its API. The move simplifies developer access to the platform's data and features.

cards.title: Riverside Podcasting Platform Enters Newsletter Publishing
cards.body: Riverside now allows users to generate AI-powered newsletters from podcast recordings. The expansion combines audio and written content creation in one platform.

cards.title: Amazon Launches $1B Frontier Deployment Engineering Organization
cards.body: Amazon created a new $1B FDE org to embed engineers within customer companies for agent deployment. The initiative mirrors OpenAI and Anthropic's enterprise strategies.

cards.title: Proton Releases Lumo 2.0 Privacy-Focused AI Chatbot
cards.body: Proton's privacy-focused chatbot Lumo 2.0 launches this week with expanded capabilities. The upgrade maintains Proton's commitment to user privacy in AI interactions.

cards.title: OKX Exchange Launches AI Agent Marketplace
cards.body: OKX developed a marketplace where AI agents can hire and pay each other. The platform combines payments, identity, and reputation systems for autonomous transactions.

cards.title: High-Intensity AI Adopters Increase Headcount 10.2%
cards.body: New research shows companies heavily adopting AI grew headcount by 10.2%, including 12% entry-level growth. The findings challenge claims that AI eliminates junior-level positions.

cards.title: Base44 Launches Own AI Model for Defensibility
cards.body: Wix-owned vibe-coding platform Base44 rolled out its proprietary AI model. The move reflects startup efforts to build defensibility beyond frontier model reliance.

cards.title: Google Expands Free Gemini Personalized Image Generation
cards.body: Gemini's personalized AI image generation now available free to eligible US users. The feature creates images based on user interests and connected Google app data.

cards.title: Anthropic and California Forge Claude Discount Deal
cards.body: Anthropic negotiated a deal allowing California state government to use Claude at 50% discount. The partnership strengthens Anthropic's position while creating federal-state friction.

cards.title: South Korea Commits $550B to Memory Chip Infrastructure
cards.body: South Korea's two largest memory chip makers pledged $550B+ to build new fabs. The investment addresses AI infrastructure demands and positions Korea as a tech powerhouse.

cards.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic's models
cards.body: The Trump administration has removed restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable model releases. This creates uncertainty across the AI industry about future regulatory guidance.

cards.title: OpenClaw launches on Android and iOS
cards.body: The free open-source agentic program is now available on mobile platforms. It brings AI agent capabilities directly to smartphone users.

cards.title: DeepMind trio builds $500M quant AI lab
cards.body: Three ex-DeepMind researchers founded EquiLibre Technologies, now valued at over $500 million. The Prague-based lab applies AI to quantitative hedge fund trading.

cards.title: Google launches Nano Banana 2 Lite image generator
cards.body: Google's new image generator is faster and cheaper than previous versions. It's designed to make AI content creation more accessible to creators.

cards.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation
cards.body: Etched, an AI chip maker competing with Nvidia, reached a $5 billion valuation with $1 billion in booked contracts. The inference-focused chip represents growing competition in AI hardware.

cards.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5
cards.body: Claude Sonnet 5 offers stronger agentic capabilities at lower pricing than Opus and Gemini Pro. The model emphasizes improved safety features for autonomous AI agents.

cards.title: Acti brings AI agents to smartphone keyboards
cards.body: Acti's new iOS and Android keyboard integrates AI assistants directly into the typing experience. Users can create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language commands.

cards.title: Anthropic launches Claude Science research workbench
cards.body: Claude Science provides scientists with an integrated environment for computational research. The tool eliminates bouncing between databases, pipelines, and separate applications.

cards.title: X launches MCP server for AI developers
cards.body: X has released a hosted MCP server to simplify AI tool integration with its API. The move makes it easier for developers to build AI applications on the platform.

cards.title: Amazon launches $1B AI agents organization
cards.body: Amazon's new Frontier Development Establishment focuses on deploying purpose-built AI agents for customers. Engineers will embed within companies to ensure fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

cards.title: Proton launches Lumo 2.0 privacy chatbot
cards.body: Lumo 2.0 expands Proton's privacy-focused AI chatbot capabilities. The upgrade provides users with broader functionality while maintaining privacy protections.

cards.title: OKX brings AI agent hiring marketplace
cards.body: OKX is combining payments, identity, and reputation systems for AI agents to hire and pay each other. The marketplace enables autonomous AI economic transactions.

cards.title: AI adoption increases entry-level job growth
cards.body: A new report finds companies with high-intensity AI adoption saw overall headcount increase 10.2%. Entry-level positions actually grew by 12% in these organizations, countering job-loss narratives.

cards.title: Base44 launches proprietary AI model
cards.body: Wix-owned vibe-coding platform Base44 is rolling out its own AI model to improve defensibility. The startup aims to eventually outperform frontier models like GPT and Claude.

cards.title: Gemini personalized image generation goes free
cards.body: Google is expanding personalized AI image generation to eligible free Gemini users in the U.S. The feature creates images based on your interests and connected Google app data.

cards.title: Anthropic and California partner on Claude pricing
cards.body: Anthropic has forged a deal allowing California government to use Claude at 50% discount. The partnership positions Anthropic closer to state institutions while federal relations remain strained.

cards.title: Claude Science supports scientific research workflows
cards.body: Anthropic announced Claude Science as a flagship product for researchers and pharmaceutical executives. The tool autonomously supports scientific research similarly to how Claude Code supports software engineering.

cards.title: AI agents are not true coworkers
cards.body: MIT Technology Review examines how companies market AI tools as 'coworkers' despite fundamental differences. The framing obscures what these systems actually are and their real capabilities.

cards.title: Enterprise AI investment reaches inflection point
cards.body: Gartner declares 2026 an 'inflection year' for aligning AI projects with business objectives. Executives increasingly turn to agentic AI to drive measurable financial returns.

cards.title: Agriculture needs AI-ready data infrastructure
cards.body: AI is transforming agriculture with promising use cases for volatile fertilizer costs and weather challenges. Industry leaders must first establish proper data groundwork before investing in AI solutions.

cards.title: Google's Nano Banana 2 Lite improves speed
cards.body: Google's latest image model iteration prioritizes speed and cost reduction over previous versions. The advancement makes AI image generation more practical for commercial applications.

cards.title: South Korea invests $550B in AI chip infrastructure
cards.body: South Korea's largest memory chip makers are committing over $550 billion to expand production capacity. The investment positions the nation as a global AI tech powerhouse.
